src package¶

Submodules¶

src.augmentation module¶

This file is meant to contain the code for augmentation

src.augmentation.random_flip(voids_df, particles_df, o_x, o_y, o_z)[source]¶

Randomly flips the sign of each axis (x, y, z) with 50% probability for both dataframes and offsets, and applies a +1 shift when flipping to account for uncentered coordinates (e.g., [0, 1] range).

Parameters: - voids_df: pd.DataFrame with columns [‘x’, ‘y’, ‘z’] - particles_df: pd.DataFrame with columns [‘x’, ‘y’, ‘z’] - o_x, o_y, o_z: float offsets

Returns: - voids_df, particles_df, new_o_x, new_o_y, new_o_z

src.augmentation.random_permute(voids_df, particles_df, o_x, o_y, o_z)[source]¶

In-place axis permutation for both dataframes and offsets by renaming columns only.

Parameters: - voids_df: pd.DataFrame with columns [‘x’, ‘y’, ‘z’] - particles_df: pd.DataFrame with columns [‘x’, ‘y’, ‘z’] - o_x, o_y, o_z: float offsets for x, y, z

Returns: - voids_df, particles_df, new_ox, new_oy, new_oz

src.augmentation.test_augmentation()[source]¶

Test the random_permute and random_flip functions with a tiny synthetic dataset.

src.dcn_config module¶

This module defines the paths for data and outputs using a standardized, deploy-ready project structure, and stores all global project-wide configuration constants.

src.dcn_config.get_dataset_paths()[source]¶

Returns the dataset paths for particles and voids for both train and val sets.

Structure expected:

project_root/ ├── data/ │ ├── voxel_particles_train/ │ ├── voxel_particles_val/ │ ├── voxel_void_train/ │ └── voxel_void_val/

src.dcn_config.get_output_paths()[source]¶

Returns the output path for models and logs.

Structure expected:

project_root/ └── output/

src.dataset module¶

This module aims to format the data at the input and output of the network with multi-anchor support and sliding window on radius subsets.

class src.dataset.VoidDataset(particles_dir, voids_dir, augment)[source]¶

Bases: Dataset

Overrides the PyTorch DataSet Class specific for our use case with multi-anchor support.

src.dataset.sort_voids_by_radius(voids)[source]¶

Sorts voids from a dataframe into subsets based on radius bins. Uses a fine-grained logarithmic scale for sliding window anchor assignment.

Parameters:

Dataframe (pandas) – voids with columns ‘x’, ‘y’, ‘z’, ‘radius’

Returns:

voids subsets sorted by radius in descending order

Return type:

list of Dataframes

src.dataset.target_to_voids(tensor, anchor, o_x, o_y, o_z, thresh=0.0)[source]¶

Opposite function of voids_to_targets. :param tensor: Tensor of shape (B, 5, grid_size, grid_size, grid_size) :param integers o_x: the offset of the cube in the universe :param o_y: the offset of the cube in the universe :param o_z: the offset of the cube in the universe :param thresh: 0.5 if after sigmoid 0.0 if before sigmoid

Returns:

dataframe of voids ‘x’, ‘y’, ‘z’, ‘radius’

Return type:

pandas Dataframe

src.dataset.void_data_loader(batch_size=1, max_train=None, max_val=None)[source]¶

Creates PyTorch DataLoader objects for training and validation datasets.

Parameters: - batch_size: int, size of each batch for data loading (default: 16) - max_train: int or None, if set limits the training set to this many samples - max_val: int or None, if set limits the validation set to this many samples

Returns: - train_loader, val_loader: two PyTorch DataLoader objects for train and validation

src.features module¶

This module contains functions to prepare multi-channel input for void detection using Gaussian smoothing at different scales. The main function, prepare_void_detection_input, takes a 3D density tensor and applies Gaussian filters with varying sigma values to create multiple feature channels. Each channel is then log-transformed and standardized to optimize it for void detection tasks.

src.features.prepare_void_detection_input(microvoxel_data)[source]¶

Prepares multi-channel input for void detection.

Parameters: - microvoxel_data: ndarray, shape (128, 128, 128), 3D density tensor obtained from np.histogramdd

Returns: - input_tensor: torch.Tensor, shape (4, 128, 128, 128), 4-channel tensor optimized for void detection at different scales

src.filter_overlap module¶

This filter mimics the pylians filter where smaller voids overlapped with bigger ones are deleted

src.filter_overlap.filter_smaller_touching_spheres(touches)[source]¶

Given a boolean matrix where touches[i, j] is True if sphere i and j overlap. Assumes spheres are ordered from largest to smallest.

src.filter_overlap.filter_touching_spheres(spheres)[source]¶

Filters out smaller spheres that touch larger ones. Assumes ‘spheres’ is a DataFrame with columns ‘x’, ‘y’, ‘z’ (optional) and ‘radius’, sorted by radius in descending order.

src.filter_overlap.run_test_and_plot(n_points=1000)[source]¶

Generates random spheres, applies the filter, and plots before/after.

src.full_grid_tensor module¶

This module contains the main function to convert void proposals into a full 3D grid tensor, and a reusable BoxClusterer class for clustering boxes based on IoU.

class src.full_grid_tensor.BoxClusterer(min_cluster_size=2, cluster_selection_epsilon=1.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Reusable clusterer for grouping boxes based on precomputed IoU matrix.

cluster(iou_mat: Tensor) → Tensor[source]¶

Cluster boxes using precomputed IoU matrix.

Parameters:

iou_mat – torch.Tensor (N,N) IoU matrix in float32

Returns:

torch.Tensor (N,) with cluster IDs

Return type:

labels

src.full_grid_tensor.group_means(df: DataFrame, labels, exclude_outliers: bool = True) → DataFrame[source]¶

Groups rows in df by cluster labels and averages values for clusters (label >= 0). Outliers (label == -1) are either dropped or appended as-is depending on exclude_outliers.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Input dataframe to group.

  • labels (np.ndarray) – Cluster labels from DBSCAN (same length as df).

  • exclude_outliers (bool, default True) – If True, outliers (label == -1) are dropped. If False, outliers are appended as-is to the result.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame with aggregated cluster means (and optionally outliers).

src.full_grid_tensor.group_means_with_outliers_(df: DataFrame, labels) → DataFrame[source]¶

Groups rows in df by cluster labels, averages values for clusters (label >= 0), and appends outliers (label == -1) as-is.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Input dataframe to group.

  • labels (np.ndarray) – Cluster labels from HDBSCAN (same length as df).

Return type:

pd.DataFrame with aggregated cluster means and ungrouped outliers.

src.full_grid_tensor.group_means_without_outliers_(df: DataFrame, labels) → DataFrame[source]¶

Groups rows in df by cluster labels, averages values for clusters (label >= 0), and DROPS outliers (label == -1) — single detections in a cell are spurious proposals.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Input dataframe to group.

  • labels (np.ndarray) – Cluster labels from DBSCAN (same length as df).

Return type:

pd.DataFrame with aggregated cluster means only (no spurious proposals).

src.full_grid_tensor.print_tensor_summary(tensor)[source]¶

Nicely print the tensor content per grid cell. Only prints active cells (confidence > 0).

src.full_grid_tensor.voids_to_target_full_grid(subset, anchor, grid_size, o_x, o_y, o_z)[source]¶

Assign every grid cell to the closest void using KDTree, and mask out cells that are outside the void radius.

Parameters:

  • subset – pandas DataFrame with columns [‘x’, ‘y’, ‘z’, ‘radius’]

  • anchor – int, anchor index (0, 1, or 2) for radius scaling

  • grid_size – int, size of the 3D output grid

  • o_x – int offsets in voxel space

  • o_y – int offsets in voxel space

  • o_z – int offsets in voxel space

Returns:

shape (5, grid_size, grid_size, grid_size)

Return type:

tensor

src.log_experiments module¶

this module saves the code at each experiment that we run to not lose track of small changes between the commits

src.log_experiments.save_code(zip_file_name, folder='.')[source]¶

Creates a zip archive of the specified folder.

Parameters: - zip_file_name: str, name of the output zip file - folder: str, path to the folder to zip (default: “.”)

Returns: - None

src.logger_matplotlib module¶

this module is meant to plot the training progress live using matplotlib

class src.logger_matplotlib.LivePlot(title='Training Progress')[source]¶

Bases: object

The class represents a matplotlib live plot for a single metric

save(folder)[source]¶

Saves the live plot to a folder :param folder: path to save the figure

update(dot, mode)[source]¶

Updates the plot :param dot: the new value to add to the plot :param mode: “train” or “val

src.logger_matplotlib.test_live_plot()[source]¶

Tests the live plot function.

Returns: - None

src.losses module¶

This module contains the definition of the loss function and main detection metrics for the yolo network with multi-anchor support

class src.losses.UncertaintyLoss(num_lambdas=3)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Automatic loss weighting using learned uncertainty.

Learns optimal weights for multiple loss components by treating task uncertainty as learnable parameters.

Reference: “Multi-Task Learning Using Uncertainty to Weigh Losses for Scene Geometry and Semantics” (Kendall et al., 2018)

forward(losses)[source]¶

Parameters: - losses: torch.Tensor of shape (num_lambdas,) containing individual losses

Returns: - torch.Tensor: scalar combined loss

Formula: L_total = Σ(1/(2sigma²) * L_i + log(sigma)) where σ² = exp(log_var)

training: bool¶
class src.losses.YOLO3DLoss(objectness_weight=1, coordinate_weight=1, radius_weight=1, focal_alpha=4, focal_gamma=2, num_anchors=3)[source]¶

Bases: Module

This class handles the hyperparameter of a Yolo-like loss function with multi-anchor support (3 anchors per detection head)

compute_single_anchor_loss(pred_anchor, target_anchor)[source]¶

Computes the loss for a single anchor.

Parameters: - pred_anchor: torch.Tensor, shape (5, grid_size, grid_size, grid_size) - target_anchor: torch.Tensor, shape (5, grid_size, grid_size, grid_size)

Returns: - dict: dictionary with losses for this anchor

focal_loss(pred, target)[source]¶

Focal Loss for objectness detection with automatic handling of logits/probabilities.

Implements the formula: FL(p_t) = -alpha_t * (1 - p_t)^gamma * CE(pred, target) where alpha_t is applied only to the positive class to encourage detection.

Parameters: - pred: torch.Tensor, shape (grid, grid, grid), predictions - If logits: unbounded values - If probabilities: values in [0,1] - target: torch.Tensor, shape (grid, grid, grid), ground truth binary values (0 or 1)

Returns: - torch.Tensor: normalized scalar focal loss value

Note: - focal_alpha > 1: pushes model towards detection (more weight to positive class) - focal_gamma > 0: reduces weight of easy examples, focuses on hard ones

forward(pred, target)[source]¶

Computes the loss for multi-anchor predictions.

Parameters: - pred: torch.Tensor, shape (15, grid_size, grid_size, grid_size) for 3 anchors or (5, grid_size, grid_size, grid_size) for backward compatibility - target: torch.Tensor, same shape as pred

Returns: - dict: dictionary with combined losses compatible with train.py

training: bool¶

src.main module¶

this module is meant to save a snapshot of the training code and start the training

src.metrics module¶

This module contains the additional detection metrics to ensure the effective match between the predicted voids and the gt ones

src.metrics.compute_accuracy(predictions, targets, thresh=0)[source]¶

Calculates the accuracy for the object score

Parameters:

  • predictions – predicted tensor (B, 5, L, L, L)

  • targets – target tensor (B, 5, L, L, L)

  • thresh – classification threshold

Returns:

dict with accuracy metrics

src.metrics.compute_cen_rad_iou_prec_rec_f1(df_pred, df_target)[source]¶

Computes: - average relative centroid error - average relative radius error - average IoU - precision - recall - f1 Pairs with distance > target radius are discarded.

src.metrics.spheres_iou(ra, rb, d)[source]¶

Compute average IoU between pairs of spheres (GPU-optimized, no boolean indexing). All computations are batched with torch.where.

src.model module¶

Cosmic Void Detection Network - Modular YOLO v1 3D with Multi-Scale Heads This implementation defines a flexible 3D detection network inspired by YOLO v1, adapted for cosmic void detection in 3D cosmological data. The architecture includes a backbone with configurable depth, a feature pyramid with fusion, and multiple detection heads.

class src.model.Config3D[source]¶

Bases: object

Configuration class for the 3D YOLO-based cosmic void detection network.

ANCHOR_MULTIPLIERS = array([1.31950791, 1.14869835, 1.        , 0.87055056, 0.75785828])¶
CHANNELS_PER_ANCHOR = 5¶
INPUT_CHANNELS = 5¶
INPUT_SIZE = (128, 128, 128)¶
NUM_ANCHORS = 5¶
SCALES = [2, 4, 8, 16]¶
TARGET_CHANNELS = 25¶
class src.model.ConvBlock3D(in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size=2, stride=2, padding=0, dropout_rate=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Reusable 3D convolutional block.

Parameters: - in_channels: int, number of input channels - out_channels: int, number of output channels - kernel_size: int, convolution kernel size (default: 2) - stride: int, convolution stride (default: 2) - padding: int, convolution padding (default: 0) - dropout_rate: float, dropout probability (default: 0.0)

Returns: - tensor: processed feature tensor

forward(x)[source]¶

Forward pass for the convolutional block.

training: bool¶
class src.model.CosmicVoidDetectionVNet(width_multiplier=2.0, depth_layers=(5, 2, 2, 2, 2), dropout_rate=0, device='cuda')[source]¶

Bases: Module

YOLO v1 3D with multi-scale heads for Void Detection. Each head has multiple anchors with their own radius multipliers Compatible with existing dataset.

Parameters: - width_multiplier: float, multiplier for network width (default: 1.0) - depth_layers: list, additional layers for each depth level (default: [1,1,1,1,1]) - dropout_rate: float, base dropout rate (default: 0.0)

Input: - tensor: shape (batch_size, INPUT_CHANNELS, 128, 128, 128)

Returns: - list: of tensors, one for each scale or head [ - Scale 2x2x2: (batch, 5*N_ANCHORS, 2, 2, 2) - Scale 4x4x4: (batch, 5*N_ANCHORS, 4, 4, 4) - Scale 8x8x8: (batch, 5*N_ANCHORS, 8, 8, 8) […] }

Each tensor has 5*N_ANCHORS channels: For each anchor there is a multiplier: [anchor*5 + 0] = objectness (void presence probability) [anchor*5 + 1] = x_offset [anchor*5 + 2] = y_offset [anchor*5 + 3] = z_offset [anchor*5 + 4] = radius_offset (log-scaled, relative to anchor)

forward(x)[source]¶

Forward pass of the cosmic void detection network.

Parameters: - x: tensor, input of shape (batch_size, INPUT_CHANNELS, 128, 128, 128)

Returns: - list: of tensors, one for each scale Each tensor has shape: (batch, 5*N_ANCHORS, grid_size, grid_size, grid_size)

initialize_for_void_detection()[source]¶

Initializes the network to optimize cosmic void detection.

This method can be called after model instantiation to apply specialized initializations.

training: bool¶
class src.model.Detection3DHead(in_channels, grid_size, dropout_rate=0.0, num_hidden_blocks=1, initial_obj_prior=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Flexible 3D detection head with multiple anchors.

Parameters: - in_channels: int, number of input channels - grid_size: int, size of the output grid - dropout_rate: float, dropout probability (default: 0.0) - num_hidden_blocks: int, number of hidden conv blocks to insert (default: 1)

Returns: - tensor: shape (5*NUM_ANCHORS, grid_size, grid_size, grid_size) For each anchor: [anchor*5 + 0] = objectness (void presence probability) [anchor*5 + 1] = x_offset [anchor*5 + 2] = y_offset [anchor*5 + 3] = z_offset [anchor*5 + 4] = radius_offset (log-scaled, relative to anchor multiplier)

forward(x)[source]¶

Forward pass for the detection head, adding the learnable prior to objectness channels.

training: bool¶
class src.model.FusionBlock3D(in_channels, out_channels)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Reusable feature fusion block.

Parameters: - in_channels: int, number of input channels - out_channels: int, number of output channels

Returns: - tensor: fused feature tensor

forward(x)[source]¶

Forward pass for the fusion block.

training: bool¶
class src.model.SuperSeparableBlock(channels, dropout_rate=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: Module

A residual hidden block: Conv3d -> BN -> LeakyReLU -> Dropout, with skip connection.

forward(x)[source]¶

Forward pass for the SuperSeparableBlock with a skip connection.

training: bool¶
class src.model.UpsampleBlock3D(in_channels, out_channels, dropout_rate=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Reusable 3D upsampling block.

Parameters: - in_channels: int, number of input channels - out_channels: int, number of output channels - dropout_rate: float, dropout probability (default: 0.0)

Returns: - tensor: upsampled feature tensor

forward(x)[source]¶

Forward pass for the upsampling block.

training: bool¶

src.profiler module¶

Lightweight background resource profiler.

Logs CPU, RAM, and GPU stats to a CSV file at a configurable interval. Uses line-buffered I/O so data survives unexpected kills.

class src.profiler.ResourceProfiler(out_dir, interval=30)[source]¶

Bases: object

Background daemon that periodically writes resource stats to a CSV.

set_epoch(epoch)[source]¶

Set the current epoch number to log.

set_note(note)[source]¶

Set a note to be logged with the next sample.

start()[source]¶

Start the profiler thread and open the CSV file.

stop()[source]¶

Stop the profiler thread and close the CSV file.

src.test_full_grid module¶

Test script for the full grid tensor conversion and related processing functions. This script creates a simple dataset with a single void, runs the full grid tensor conversion, and prints the resulting tensor summary and predicted voids after clustering.

src.test_full_grid.test_voids_to_target_functions()[source]¶

Test function for voids_to_target_full_grid and related processing functions.

src.train module¶

Training code for the cosmic void 3D detector.

Refactored into composable functions:

process_head → single head, single sample process_sample → all heads for one sample process_batch → forward + loss for one batch run_epoch → full train or val epoch train_detector → outer training loop

Plus:

TrainingConfig → all hyperparameters in one place ModelCheckpointer → save/load logic

class src.train.ModelCheckpointer(out_folder, exp_name)[source]¶

Bases: object

Handles saving, loading, and best-model tracking.

static find_latest_experiment(out_folder)[source]¶

Return the most recent experiment folder name, or None.

static load_or_init(model, out_folder, last_exp='auto')[source]¶

Try full load → partial load (strict=False) → fresh init. If last_exp=”auto”, picks the latest folder containing Best_model.pth.

save_if_best(model, loss)[source]¶

Save the model if the given loss is better than the best seen so far.

save_last(model)[source]¶

Save the last model state (overwrites every epoch).

class src.train.TrainingConfig(lr: float = 5e-05, epochs: int = 10, batch_size: int = 1, weight_decay: float = 0.01, hwem: int = 2, n_anchors: int = 3, cube_size: float = 125.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Holds all training hyper-parameters in one place.

batch_size: int = 1¶
cube_size: float = 125.0¶
epochs: int = 10¶
hwem: int = 2¶
lr: float = 5e-05¶
n_anchors: int = 3¶
weight_decay: float = 0.01¶
src.train.backward_step(total_loss, optimizer, gscaler, model, config)[source]¶

Back-propagation with mixed-precision scaling and gradient clipping.

src.train.decode_and_fuse(tensor, sample_meta, config, box_fuser)[source]¶

Decode all anchors from a raw tensor, apply WBF and overlap filtering.

Works identically for both predictions and targets.

Returns a filtered DataFrame of decoded voids.

src.train.export_onnx(model, exp_name)[source]¶

Optional ONNX export (kept separate from training).

src.train.process_batch(batch, model, loss_fn, config, box_fuser, logger, device)[source]¶

Forward pass + per-sample loss accumulation for one batch.

Returns the total (weighted) loss tensor.

src.train.process_head(pred, target, sample_meta, config, box_fuser, loss_fn)[source]¶

Evaluate a single detection head for one sample.

Returns:

  • head_loss (Tensor | None) – Scalar loss on GPU (None when no ground-truth voids exist).

  • metrics (dict) – All metrics needed by the logger.

src.train.process_sample(preds, targets_list, sample_id, batch, config, box_fuser, loss_fn, logger)[source]¶

Iterate over all heads for one sample inside a batch.

Returns the accumulated (weighted) loss across heads.

src.train.run_epoch(epoch, mode, loader, model, loss_fn, optimizer, gscaler, config, box_fuser, logger, checkpointer, device)[source]¶

Execute one full epoch (train or validation).

src.train.start_all(exp_name='voids_3d_detection')[source]¶

Main entry point to start training.

src.train.train_detector(model, train_loader, valid_loader, config, exp_name='train_detector')[source]¶

Main training loop.

Parameters:

  • model – network to train

  • train_loader – training DataLoader

  • valid_loader – validation DataLoader

  • config – TrainingConfig with all hyper-parameters

  • exp_name – experiment name (used for logging / checkpoints)

src.train.wbf(df, grid_s, box_fuser, cube_size=125.0)[source]¶

Apply Weighted Box Fusion to a DataFrame of voids.

src.train_logger module¶

this module aims to handle all the logging of the metrics and the plot of the training curves for the project

class src.train_logger.AverageValueMeter[source]¶

Bases: object

Meter to keep track of average values during training, such as losses and metrics.

add(value, n=1)[source]¶

Add a new value to the meter.

reset()[source]¶

Reset the meter to initial state.

value()[source]¶

Return the average and current value.

class src.train_logger.TrainingLogger(exp_name, n_heads=5, n_samples=16)[source]¶

Bases: object

This class handles all the log of the metrics during the training

all_keys()[source]¶

Returns: all the metric keys short description

all_titles()[source]¶

Returns: all the metric extended description

det_update(key, head, sample_id, value)[source]¶

Updates a single value in the matrix of a specific metric :param key: the metric to update :param head: the head where is measured :param sample_id: the id of the sample where is measured :param value: the value of the metric

get_meter(key)[source]¶

Returns the meter for a specific metric

log(epoch, mode)[source]¶

Log e crea grafici per l’epoca corrente

log_plt(mode)[source]¶

Calculates the global averages of the metrics and passes them to the plotter :param mode: “train” or “val”

print_all(epoch, mode)[source]¶

Prints all the metrics :param epoch: epoch number :param mode: “train” or “val”

reset()[source]¶

Resets the logger

save_plots()[source]¶

Saves the training graphs

save_txt()[source]¶

Salva un riepilogo finale

update(key)[source]¶

Updates the global average of a specific metric based on the detailed values Args: key: the metric to update

update_all()[source]¶

Updates all the global averages of the metrics based on the detailed values
